info.name: Wingz Ride API
info.description:
Read-only Django REST API for the Wingz ride management assessment. Covers JWT authentication and the `/api/rides/` list + retrieve endpoints. Works against both a local Django runserver (http://localhost:8000) and the deployed EC2 instance (https://wingz-ride.d3sarrollo.dev).

## Quick start

1. Import this collection.
2. Import **one** environment file from the same folder:
   - `wingz-api.local.postman_environment.json` for a local runserver
   - `wingz-api.prod.postman_environment.json` for the deployed API
3. Select the environment from Postman's top-right dropdown.
4. Run **Auth → Login** — the `access_token` and `refresh_token` are captured automatically into the active environment.
5. Every other request is now authenticated via `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` (inherited from the collection).

## Notes

- Access tokens last **15 minutes**. Re-run **Login** or **Refresh token** when a request returns 401.
- The `/api/rides/` endpoints require `role=admin`. The seed admin (`admin@wingz.com` / `adminpass123`) already satisfies this.
- The API is **read-only** — there are no POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints for rides.
- Seed data is reset on every deploy, so `rideId=1` and `rider_email=alice@example.com` are always valid on the deployed instance.

item.name: Auth

request.description:
Exchange email + password for a JWT pair.

**Success (200)**
```json
{
  "access_token": "<jwt>",
  "refresh_token": "<jwt>",
  "expires_in": 900,
  "user": {
    "id": 1,
    "email": "admin@wingz.com",
    "role": "admin",
    "first_name": "Admin",
    "last_name": "User"
  }
}
```

The test script stores `access_token` + `refresh_token` in the active environment.

**Errors**
- `400` — missing `email` or `password`
- `401` — invalid credentials

Exchange a refresh token for a new access token. The refresh token itself is NOT rotated — it stays valid until its original 7-day expiry.

**Success (200)**
```json
{
  "access_token": "<jwt>",
  "expires_in": 900
}
```

The test script overwrites `{{accessToken}}` in the active environment.

**Errors**
- `400` — missing `refresh_token`
- `401` — invalid or expired refresh token

Return the currently authenticated user's profile. Requires a valid bearer token.

**Success (200)**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "email": "admin@wingz.com",
  "role": "admin",
  "first_name": "Admin",
  "last_name": "User"
}
```

**Errors**
- `401` — missing or invalid token

Stateless courtesy endpoint — the server does not track issued tokens, so real invalidation happens client-side (drop both tokens from storage). Returns `204 No Content` when called with a valid bearer token.

**Errors**
- `401` — missing or invalid token

item.description: JWT authentication flow. Run **Login** first; tokens are captured into the environment automatically and every other request inherits `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` from the collection.

item.name: Rides

request.description:
List rides with default pagination (page 1, page_size 10) and no filters.

**Response envelope**
```json
{
  "count": 24,
  "next": "http://.../api/rides/?page=2",
  "previous": null,
  "results": [
    {
      "id_ride": 1,
      "status": "to-pickup",
      "id_rider": { "id_user": ..., "email": "...", "role": "rider", "first_name": "...", "last_name": "...", "phone_number": "..." },
      "id_driver": { ... },
      "pickup_latitude": 14.5995,
      "pickup_longitude": -90.5131,
      "dropoff_latitude": 14.60,
      "dropoff_longitude": -90.51,
      "pickup_time": "2026-04-10T15:00:00Z",
      "todays_ride_events": [ { "id_ride_event": 1, "description": "Ride requested", "created_at": "..." } ]
    }
  ]
}
```

`todays_ride_events` only includes events from the last 24 hours (filtered at the DB level via a `Prefetch`).

**Errors**
- `401` — missing or invalid token
- `403` — authenticated user is not an admin

query.description: Items per page. Default: 10. Max: 100 (larger values are silently capped).

request.description: Demonstrates the `page` + `page_size` query params. `next`/`previous` in the response envelope point at the adjacent pages.

query.description: Filter by ride status. Valid values: `to-pickup`, `en-route`, `dropoff`.

request.description: Filter rides by status. Try swapping `status` to `to-pickup` or `dropoff` to see the other lifecycle phases.

query.description: Exact-match filter on the rider's email address. The seed DB gives Alice 8 rides.

request.description: Filter rides by rider email (exact match). Against seed data, `alice@example.com` returns 8 rides.
`status` and `rider_email` can be combined — both filters are applied.

query.description: Sort key. Valid values: `pickup_time`, `distance` (distance also requires `latitude` + `longitude`).

request.description: Sort rides by `pickup_time` ascending. Without a `sort_by`, results are ordered by `id_ride` (the model default).

query.description: Sort by Haversine distance from (latitude, longitude). Distance is computed at the database level so ORDER BY + pagination work on the full dataset.
Reference latitude in decimal degrees. Required when sort_by=distance.
Reference longitude in decimal degrees. Required when sort_by=distance.

request.description:
Sort by proximity to a reference point (here: Guatemala City, matching `ride0/tests/test_deployed_api.sh` so Postman and the shell regression cover the same cases). Nearest rides come first.

Change `latitude`/`longitude` to any valid decimal-degree pair to re-sort around a different reference point.

Validation case — `sort_by=distance` without `latitude`/`longitude` must return 400 with `{"error": "latitude and longitude are required for distance sorting"}`. The test script asserts both the status and the error message.

Fetch a single ride by primary key (`id_ride`). Returns the same nested shape as a single list result.

Change the `rideId` environment variable to fetch a different ride.

**Errors**
- `404` — ride does not exist
- `401` / `403` — auth issues

item.description: List + retrieve against `/api/rides/`. All requests inherit `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}` from the collection and require the authenticated user to have `role=admin`.
